key_insights.insight: Charisma and confidence are learnable skills, not inherent traits, that dramatically impact both external success and internal well-being.
key_insights.quote: So it is most definitely something you can learn. I feel like I'm a testament to that.

key_insights.insight: Shifting one's environment can be highly impactful in changing social habits and developing confidence.
key_insights.quote: shifting your environment is so impactful, probably one of the most impactful things.

key_insights.insight: Before expressing interest in someone, establish fun, trust, and respect in the first 60 seconds to flip the dynamic.
key_insights.quote: If you, and this can take 60 seconds or less, can communicate that you are fun, that the person can trust you, and that there is something to respect about you, and then you express your interest in that person, it will completely flip the dynamic on its head.

key_insights.insight: Instead of focusing solely on being interested in others to make a good first impression, establish your own value first.
key_insights.quote: Just the best way to get people interested in you is to be interested in them. And what I found is that that is generally true, but it ignores the realities of status...

key_insights.insight: It's important to take the risk of overshooting and being seen, because most people consistently undershoot what is available to them in terms of creating connection.
key_insights.quote: Everyone's fears of overshooting, but they spend almost all of their lives undershooting.

key_insights.insight: To combat autopilot responses in conversations, reverse engineer the conversation you want to have and leave 'crumbs' to guide the interaction.
key_insights.quote: reverse engineer the conversation that you would like to have... to take the three topics that you know you'd love to talk about... and find a way to just leave little crumbs in these common answers that give the right person the invitation to talk to you about that thing.

key_insights.insight: Slow movements convey higher status because they communicate safety and comfort in the environment.
key_insights.quote: It's safety. I mean, prey animals are hunted and that's what you have to be head on a swivel. If you're a prey animal, you want to move slow, you're dead. It's over.

key_insights.insight: It is vital to integrate authenticity into leadership and interactions, but when you reach a level of public power, be wary of the transactional relationships that can arise and be discerning.
key_insights.quote: as you grow, and hopefully become more powerful in the world, the level of deception and the level of even just in the lightest like people trying to please you and make you happy is going to escalate. And you're going to need to develop a discernment

key_insights.insight: The essence of confidence is recognizing that there are no superiors, only people with similar needs for connection and joy.
key_insights.quote: when you realize...You're not dealing with roles.You're not dealing with investors.You're not dealing with avatars of beauty.You are dealing with people and the people that they love the most in their life

key_insights.insight: It's helpful to actively share your purpose to make people aware of who you are. Not the fake one, the one that's so real that people want to participate.
key_insights.quote: People like to participate in that. They like to give help in order to do that.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice 'one more sentence' in daily interactions.
actionable_advice.how_to: Whenever you interact with someone (Uber driver, shop assistant, etc.), say one sentence more than you normally would. For example, instead of just saying 'What floor?' in an elevator, add 'Have you lived in this building for a long time?'

actionable_advice.advice: Before interviews, get into an expansive state by opening your body language and doing an affirmation.
actionable_advice.how_to: Before you get into a networking event or an interview, find a bathroom or a spot where you can stretch out your body. Signal to your body that you are safe by raising your hands above your head. While doing that, say 'I approve of me' or 'I love myself' to build this physical body energy connection.

actionable_advice.advice: In casual conversation, reverse engineer what you want to talk about to leave conversational "crumbs".
actionable_advice.how_to: When asked a common question (e.g., 'Where are you from?'), provide an answer that includes three topics you'd love to discuss, giving the other person multiple 'hooks' to connect with you on. For example, 'I grew up in Philadelphia, but I never really fit in there, so I traveled throughout my 20s and now live in LA because it's the right place for my business.'

When speaking, intentionally pause instead of using 'um' or 'uh' to create a vacuum that draws attention. Record yourself telling a story and identify filler words; then consciously replace them with silence in future conversations.

actionable_advice.advice: Communicate standards to be attractive and break out of the purely physical dynamic.
actionable_advice.how_to: Make a mental list of qualities beyond physical appearance you value in potential partners.  In conversations, make statements that reflect your interest and highlight when they fulfill those qualities. Show you are making a genuine evaluation, not being automatically interested.  Express that you had certain expectations of what qualities a person should have, and they met or exceeded them.

memorable_stories: Charlie's story of breaking out of his shell while studying abroad.
The story of his brother using the name 'Dragon' at a networking event, highlighting the power of playfulness.
The story of the band the Buried Life playing basketball with Obama.
A recollection of Charlie going on a psychedelic journey to find greater self-acceptance.
contrarian_claims: Being genuinely interested in others, a common piece of advice, is insufficient for making a good first impression.
In mass media environments, sometimes an apology will only add fuel to fire and create more of a problem than it resolves.
It is better to overshare and risk vulnerability than to undershoot and miss connections
It can sometimes be more advantageous to attack over defending.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Charisma Teacher: Psychology Of Why People Don't Like You! People Are Attracted To These Traits! The Secret To Being Instantly Likeable!
